cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure out this question. So the task is to determine which statement is NOT an advantage of the MIT algorithm described in the passage. The options are A, B, C, or D. It can provide two seconds' warning if another car is about to cross a crossroads, giving other drivers time to brake. 3. Jonathan How mentions that future "smart" cars might use this to help avoid accidents. 4. The algorithm was tested in Virginia and accurately identified violators within a couple of seconds of the red light. 5. Compared to other models, it has fewer false alarms, which is important for driver guidance systems. 6. The findings are published in a transportation systems journal. Now, looking at the options: A) It will raise the alarm when the driver breaks the traffic rules. - The passage mentions that the monitor "guesses" which cars are likely to break the Highway Code and provides warnings. So this seems like an advantage. So A is an advantage, not the correct answer. B) It usually produces fewer false alarms. - The text explicitly says, "Compared to other efforts... the MIT algorithm brought fewer false alarms..." So B is definitely an advantage. Not the correct answer. C) It helps drivers avoid potential accidents by judging the threats. - The system provides warnings (two seconds) which helps drivers react. Jonathan How also mentions that smart cars could use this to avoid accidents. Therefore, C is an advantage. Not the correct answer. D) The monitor of this algorithm can judge the traffic condition from the lights of other vehicles. - The passage says the monitor "watches" deceleration and distance from a light. It doesn't say it judges traffic conditions based on the lights of other vehicles. The system is monitoring the vehicle's own deceleration and distance, not the lights (like brake lights or turn signals) of other vehicles. So D might not be an advantage mentioned in the text.
